questions.text: You’ve got the entire day to yourself. What do you, as an aspiring pop star do?

choices.text: Check the news, see what other pop stars are up to and how people react to that.
choices.comment: Dull, but shrewd. You gotta learn the terrain. Even if you’re gonna do something completely different.

choices.text: Phone your wildest mates and hit the hottest club within taxi range.
choices.comment: Nice work if you can afford it! But if you can get in, it’ll get you seen.

choices.text: Do a bit of homework: what’s tranding now; what was trending last month, and the month before that? What do people think is coming next?
choices.comment: Someone’s trying to be clever! But a bit of planning can pay high dividends. ... ’Ere, are you after my job or something?

choices.text: Write some songs.
choices.comment: Are you kidding me? I’ll tell you right now, those songs are gonna be rubbish. We’ll find some proper ones for you to sing.

questions.text: You’ve just been asked what you think about the latest song by WHOEVER. You haven’t heard it and don’t have an opinion. DO YOU:

choices.text: Admit just that.
choices.comment: Well, at least you’re honest, I suppose. People will respect that. If you don’t put them to sleep first!

choices.text: Gush about it like it’s the best thing you’ve ever heard.
choices.comment: Enthusiasm’s great, especially if you can generate it on demand.

choices.text: Say it’s rubbish, and say something nastier about WHOEVER.
choices.comment: Even if it makes people less likely to want to work with you, Johnny Public likes a bit of drama!

choices.text: Pivot to talking about stuff you do like.
choices.comment: You might not give the interview THEY want, but you gave the interview YOU want.

questions.text: It’s time to work on your debut single! What’s it going to be?

choices.text: Something brilliant and ambitious and artistic.
choices.comment: HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HAAAA! Oh I’m sorry, you’re serious, aren’t you?

choices.text: A novelty, something to do with a current meme that the kids love right now and the adults hate. Catchy. But annoying. But catchy. But annoying.
choices.comment: Well, of course it’s beneath you! It’s beneath everyone! But it’ll get you noticed.

choices.text: A cover of a song that everyone likes; a classic.
choices.comment: Just don’t make it one of the songs I like: they’ve been ruined enough already!

choices.text: A cover of something godawful that was popular when it came out and somehow still has nostalgia value.
choices.comment: Well, you can’t make it worse, so maybe you’re onto something here!

questions.text: An interviewer asks when your birthday is. Do you:

choices.text: Just tell her.
choices.comment: They sing! They dance! They answer simple questions about themselves!

choices.text: Tease; ask when hers is.
choices.comment: Turn the table, flip the script, make the interviewer remember you.

choices.text: Tell her a well-practised lie.
choices.comment: Assuming you’ve got a well-practiced lie to tell! Your legend is yours to build.

choices.text: Ask what the Hell that has to do with the release, get increasingly irritable and flippant throughout the rest of the interview.
choices.comment: Tell me, at some point do you expect people to actually LIKE you?

questions.text: It’s time to look at your image. What’s your approach?

choices.text: Image isn’t important. Music is. I’ll wear what I want, it doesn’t matter.
choices.comment: Here’s a funny fact: did you know most of the world has eyes?

choices.text: Whatever’s usual for my scene.
choices.comment: Safe enough, I suppose. But trends are to be set, not followed.

choices.text: What’s going to stand out and get me noticed?
choices.comment: Good question! Let’s just hope it won’t get us sued too.

choices.text: Something dignified. Smart or casual or costume, just so long as no-one takes the mickey.
choices.comment: Good answer... if a bit timid. You're meant to be a pop star. Stick your neck out a bit!

questions.text: An interviewer asks you to define your music.

choices.text: A carefully considered answer that shows that, new or not, you know your music and where yours fits in.
choices.comment: You’ll be telling me you can actually play an instrument next!

choices.text: “I dunno, I’m just here to have some fun. Have we got to categorise everything?”
choices.comment: Ooh, the ‘tired of music journalism’ angle. Interesting!

choices.text: “The only music you need to listen to, ‘cause everything else is frankly rubbish.”
choices.comment: Sometimes pop needs its arrogant young upstarts. Can you pull it off, though?

choices.text: Make up some term that nobody’s heard of and fewer people understand, and hope the phrase catches on.
choices.comment: Acid Skiffle? No, wait. Ambient thrash. Hardcore Easy-Listening Techno? Dirty House? So many options...

questions.text: You’ve been asked to condense your entire look down to one accessory. What is it?

choices.text: An eyepatch.
choices.comment: Eyepatches always look stupid if you don't need one, but they always come back into style.

choices.text: A piercing.
choices.comment: Old folks hate ’em, young folks love ’em, but who’s indifferent to a weird piercing?

choices.text: A pair of really tight trousers.
choices.comment: Whether now while you’re young, or years later when you’re old and have put on weight, they’ll notice!

choices.text: A scruffy T-shirt featuring a band that split up before you were born.
choices.comment: A bargain at 300 quid!

questions.text: You’ve blagged your way onto one of the big talent shows. You’re going to sing. What do you sing for the judges?

choices.text: A standard, one of the judges’ favourites.
choices.comment: On the one hand, that'll ruin the song for the judge. On the other, I bloody hate that judge, probably.

choices.text: Something a bit rougher or a bit funkier; something that’s a bit more forgiving of the flaws in my own voice.
choices.comment: Sensible. And you gotta do something you’re comfortable with. The challenge is putting up with what they say after!

choices.text: It doesn’t matter so long as I put all my heart and soul into it.
choices.comment: You sound just like all the other contestants now! The rubbish ones, mind you.

choices.text: Something stupid and moronic, but have it change gear halfway through into a completely different style. It might be crap, but they won’t know quite what to think.
choices.comment: I like it! Don’t be good if you can be great. And don’t be crap if you can be AWFUL,

questions.text: An interviewer asks you what book you last read, and your opinion of it. What do you pick?

choices.text: Something topical and dangerous and revolutionary, but then claim it put you to sleep.
choices.comment: Ooh, tricksy! Tempt them with the possibility of your intelligence then leave ‘em guessing!

choices.text: Something clever and profound, and offer a critique.
choices.comment: Okay, so you’re smart, but be careful: you don’t want to alienate the punters!

choices.text: Something popular and simple, but excuse it as a guilty pleasure, or defend it, asking them what’s wrong with a bit of comfort reading?
choices.comment: See, now they identify with you. Just don't try to be too common. People know when you’re faking that.

choices.text: A kid’s book, and play up your mischievous side by saying you found it too difficult.
choices.comment: The ‘am I really this stupid?’ ploy? Cunning! But pick the right kid’s book and make sure you know which bits give you difficulty!

questions.text: You’ve got another day to yourself, and you feel you should do something to improve your career. What do you do?

choices.text: Rehearse.
choices.comment: You’re always going to have something to rehearse, even if it's nothing particularly worthy.

choices.text: Take a singing lesson.
choices.comment: Famous singers don’t need to sound good! But on the other hand... it DOES save on studio time.

choices.text: Go to the market, see if you can find something that’ll look good on stage.
choices.comment: Stranger things have happened, so keep your eyes open! Oh, and pick us up some prawns from the fish stall, will you?

choices.text: Go where someone famous is known to drink, then play the most immature prank you can and stream it.
choices.comment: Oh Gawd, I can feel a headache coming on. Just don't get arrested or do something he could sue you for, okay?
